expert_rss.title: The U.S. Waits Out the Iran War, Eyeing Potential Intervention in Cuba
expert_rss.summary:
On July 20, the U.S. Department of State issued a nine-section report, titled “Cuba: the Capital of 21st Century Communism,” that purported a decades-long Cuban espionage and subversion operation to export the communist revolution to the U.S. through the use of left-wing terrorists, guerrilla networks, spies, and influence actors. The report largely reviews Cuban political […]

expert_rss.title: A Year of Monitoring: Anti-Data Center Narratives and Threats
expert_rss.summary:
The state of New York has established the United States’ first statewide moratorium on hyperscale data centers, framed as a bid to keep residents’ electricity bills down and protect environmental resources. Legislative proposals elsewhere similarly seek to slow the buildup of computational infrastructure that has become an important part of AI research, development, and deployment. […]

expert_rss.title: The Montreal Manifesto: An Attempt to Intellectualise Incel Ideology
expert_rss.summary: On the morning of 22 June 2026, police responded to a report of a gun barrel visible in a hotel window in the Côte-des-Neiges neighbourhood of Montreal, Canada. Ensuing gunfire between police and the perpetrator left one civilian, one police officer, and the attacker dead.
